Access to Field Operations SharePoint is revoked while Cyber Policy Teams channel, Agency Policy Repository, and Moderate-Security Azure enclave are provisioned instantly. Conditional Access updates to require phishing-resistant MFA (PIV Card) for policy-specific resources. The Sync Orchestrator updates her ServiceNow CI record and notifies the Cyber Policy Lead via MS Teams. metrics: time_to_productivity: "Reduced from 5-7 days to less than 60 minutes" security_risk: "Eliminated access creep from accumulated permissions" admin_load: "100% reduction in manual group management for transfers" leaver_scenario: persona: "James Vance, Contractor" event: "Immediate Separation (End of Contract / High-Risk Departure)" narrative: > The Leaver scenario demonstrates the automated kill switch. When HR finalizes the separation, the HRIS pushes a status update setting Employee Status to Terminated with immediate effect. Entra ID disables the account within seconds, blocking all new sign-in sessions across M365, Azure, ServiceNow, and federated apps. Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) issues a revocation signal to all supporting apps, terminating active sessions instantly without waiting for token expiry. The Sync Orchestrator moves all assigned assets (laptop, PIV card, mobile) to In Stock Pending Recovery status in ServiceNow and generates a high-priority recovery ticket for Physical Security. Sentinel logs the disablement and monitors for failed login attempts. A final Atlas Report is sent to the Department Lead and Security via MS Teams. metrics: revocation_latency: "Reduced from hours/days to less than 120 seconds" audit_compliance: "100% automated logging for FISMA and GCC-Moderate separation" asset_accountability: "Zero-latency flagging of agency hardware for recovery"

docs.title: UIAO Unified Architecture
docs.text: Version 1.0

docs.title: 1. Architectural Overview
docs.text:
The Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay Architecture (UIAO) is a modernization initiative designed to unify identity, addressing, routing, telemetry, and governance into a coherent, Zero Trust-aligned federal architecture. It integrates Microsoft Entra ID as the identity control plane, ICAM as the governance backbone, InfoBlox as the authoritative IPAM, Cisco SD-WAN as the routing control plane, and cloud-native telemetry and location services as the truth source for operational decisions. Together, these components form a coordinated modernization effort that replaces fragmented legacy systems with a cloud-optimized, identity-driven, telemetry-informed enterprise. The strategic goal is to transform the agency into a modern federal network where identity is the perimeter, telemetry is the truth, routing is cloud-first, and governance is automated. UIAO provides the architectural foundation needed to meet Zero Trust expectations, TIC 3.0 requirements, and FedRAMP-aligned controls while improving mission performance and citizen experience.

The Unified Identity-Addressing-Overlay (UIAO) architecture integrates four control planes into a single cohesive framework. Each plane governs a distinct operational domain while sharing telemetry, policy signals, and configuration data with the others through well-defined integration points.

docs.title: 2. Four-Plane Architecture Diagram
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates the complete UIAO architecture, showing how the Identity, Addressing, Overlay, and Telemetry control planes interconnect through the central Policy Engine.

docs.title: 3.1. Identity Control Plane
docs.text: The Identity Control Plane is anchored in Entra ID and reinforced by ICAM governance, Conditional Access, Privileged Identity Management, and lifecycle automation. Identity becomes the authoritative source for access, addressing, certificates, and policy.

docs.title: 3.2. Network Control Plane
docs.text: The Network Control Plane uses Cisco SD-WAN to deliver cloud-first routing, performance-optimized paths for M365, and identity-aware segmentation. Integration with INR enables location-aware routing and emergency services readiness.

docs.title: 3.3. Addressing Control Plane
docs.text: The Addressing Control Plane modernizes IPAM through InfoBlox, replacing spreadsheets with authoritative, identity-derived addressing. DNS and DHCP are unified across cloud and on-prem environments, enabling consistent policy enforcement and accurate telemetry correlation.

docs.title: 3.4. Telemetry & Location Control Plane
docs.text: The Telemetry and Location Control Plane consolidates signals from M365, SD-WAN, endpoints, DNS, CDM/CLAW, and SIEM platforms. Telemetry becomes a real-time control input for routing, security, and compliance, enabling conversation-level visibility across the enterprise.

docs.title: 3.5. Security & Compliance Plane
docs.text: The Security and Compliance Plane aligns the architecture with TIC 3.0, Zero Trust, FedRAMP 20x Phase 2, NIST 800-63, and ICAM governance. Security becomes embedded in the architecture rather than bolted on, with automated enforcement replacing manual review.

docs.title: 4. Multi-Plane Integration
docs.text: The UIAO architecture achieves its power through deep integration between control planes. The telemetry plane provides closed-loop feedback to all other planes, enabling real-time policy adjustment and continuous compliance validation.

docs.title: 5. Core Stack Integration
docs.text: The following diagram illustrates how the three pillars of the UIAO framework interconnect. Each node is derived from the program data layer.

docs.title: 6. Legacy vs. Modernized State
docs.text: The following diagram compares the fragmented legacy state with the unified modernized architecture that UIAO delivers.

docs.title: 7. Mission-to-Technology Mapping
docs.text: The following diagram demonstrates how each technical control plane maps to strategic mission outcomes, providing executive visibility into the return on modernization investment.

docs.title: 8.1. Conversation as the Atomic Unit
docs.text: Every interaction—identity, certificate, addressing, path, QoS, and telemetry—is treated as a single, correlated conversation rather than isolated events.

docs.title: 8.2. Identity as the Root Namespace
docs.text: Identity becomes the root namespace for all resources, ensuring that every IP address, certificate, subnet, policy, and telemetry event is derived from or bound to identity.

docs.title: 8.3. Deterministic Addressing
docs.text: Addressing becomes deterministic and policy-driven, replacing ad-hoc assignment with identity-derived logic that enables accurate correlation and automated governance.

docs.title: 8.4. Certificate-Anchored Overlay
docs.text: Certificates and mutual TLS anchor tunnels, services, and trust relationships across the enterprise.

docs.title: 8.5. Telemetry as Control
docs.text: Telemetry becomes an active control input for routing, security, and compliance decisions rather than a passive reporting mechanism.

docs.title: 8.6. Embedded Governance & Automation
docs.text: Governance is executed through orchestrated workflows that enforce policy consistently and reduce operational burden.

docs.title: 8.7. Public Service First
docs.text: Citizen experience, accessibility, and privacy remain top-level design constraints.

docs.title: 9. Jinja2 Normalization Pipeline
docs.text: All UIAO configuration data flows through a normalization pipeline that ensures consistency between YAML data sources and Jinja2 templates.

docs.title: 10. Executive Dashboard Status
docs.text:
The following diagram provides a real-time view of deployment readiness across all UIAO components.

Generated from UIAO data layer — Unified Architecture v1.0

docs.title: UIAO Vendor Technology Stack and Compliance Registry
docs.text: Classification: CUI/FOUO Version: 1.0 Generated: Auto-compiled from data/vendor-stack.yml

docs.title: 1. Purpose
docs.text:
This document provides a comprehensive registry of the core technology vendors that comprise the UIAO architecture. It serves three critical functions within the Documentation-as-Code pipeline: it tracks each vendor's FedRAMP authorization status, maps vendor capabilities to NIST 800-53 Rev 5 controls, and monitors compliance with active CISA directives including Binding Operational Directives and Emergency Directives.

Unlike traditional vendor documentation maintained in static Word files or spreadsheets, this registry is machine-generated from structured YAML data stored in the repository. When a vendor releases a critical patch, updates its FedRAMP certification, or when CISA issues a new directive, a single update to data/vendor-stack.yml automatically regenerates this document, the FedRAMP Crosswalk, and all downstream compliance artifacts.

docs.title: 2. How This Document Works
docs.text:
The vendor stack document is produced through the UIAO Document Compiler pipeline, which operates as follows:

  1. Data Layer: The structured vendor data resides in data/vendor-stack.yml. Each vendor entry includes its product name, UIAO pillar mapping, FedRAMP certification class, required software versions, applicable NIST controls, KSI category mapping, and any active CISA directive compliance notes.

  2. Template Layer: This Jinja2 template (templates/vendor_stack_v1.0.md.j2) defines the narrative structure and formatting. It uses Jinja2 loops and conditionals to iterate over the vendor entries and render them into readable Markdown.

  3. Generation Engine: The scripts/generate_docs.py Python script loads the canon YAML and all data/*.yml files, merges them into a unified context dictionary, and passes that context to each Jinja2 template. The template engine replaces variable placeholders with actual data values.

  4. CI/CD Pipeline: On every push to the main branch, a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/docs.yml) triggers the generation script. The output is written to both docs/ and site/ directories, then committed back to the repository automatically.

  5. Website Delivery: The GitHub Pages site at https://whalermike.github.io/uiao-core/ reads the generated Markdown from the site/ directory and renders it through the USWDS-styled Document Compiler interface, where it can be viewed inline or downloaded in Markdown, Word, PDF, or HTML formats.

docs.title: 3. Core Vendor Registry
docs.text:
The UIAO architecture integrates five primary technology vendors, each mapped to a specific architectural pillar. Under FedRAMP 20x (2026 Consolidated Rules), all vendors are classified using the new Class-based system, where Class C corresponds to the legacy Moderate baseline.

docs.title: 6. Maintaining This Document
docs.text:
This document is automatically regenerated whenever changes are pushed to the main branch. To update vendor information:

  1. Edit data/vendor-stack.yml with the new vendor version, directive status, or certification update.
  2. Commit and push to main.
  3. The GitHub Actions workflow will regenerate all documents, including this one.
  4. The updated document will appear on the UIAO-Core website within minutes.

This approach ensures that the vendor compliance registry is never stale, always version-controlled, and permanently auditable through Git history.

docs.title: UIAO Zero Trust Narrative
docs.text: Version 1.0

docs.title: Zero Trust Imperative
docs.text: The agency’s current environment is constrained by legacy TIC 2.0 routing patterns that force traffic through centralized bottlenecks, degrading performance and limiting cloud adoption. Identity remains anchored in on-premises Active Directory, creating governance gaps and inconsistent enforcement across divisions. Addressing is fragmented across spreadsheets and disconnected IPAM tools, making it difficult to correlate identity, device, and network activity. Telemetry is incomplete and siloed, preventing conversation-level visibility and limiting the agency’s ability to support INR, E911, or Zero Trust enforcement. These limitations have direct mission impact. M365 performance is degraded by unnecessary hairpinning. Cyber risk increases when identity governance is inconsistent and telemetry is incomplete. Compliance gaps emerge when the agency cannot meet TIC 3.0, FedRAMP 20x Phase 2, or SCuBA expectations. Operational inefficiencies multiply when governance depends on manual tickets instead of automated workflows. Modernization is required to support mission readiness, cyber resilience, and citizen-facing services.

docs.title: Identity as the Perimeter
docs.text: The Identity Control Plane is anchored in Entra ID and reinforced by ICAM governance, Conditional Access, Privileged Identity Management, and lifecycle automation. Identity becomes the authoritative source for access, addressing, certificates, and policy.

docs.title: Telemetry as Truth
docs.text: The Telemetry and Location Control Plane consolidates signals from M365, SD-WAN, endpoints, DNS, CDM/CLAW, and SIEM platforms. Telemetry becomes a real-time control input for routing, security, and compliance, enabling conversation-level visibility across the enterprise.

docs.title: Governance as Automation
docs.text: Governance is executed through orchestrated workflows that enforce policy consistently and reduce operational burden.

docs.title: Zero Trust Outcomes
docs.text:
UIAO delivers measurable improvements across performance, security, compliance, and mission readiness. Cloud-first routing and identity- driven segmentation reduce latency and improve M365 performance. Stronger identity governance and deterministic addressing enhance Zero Trust enforcement. Unified telemetry enables accurate location inference, conversation-level visibility, and real-time decision-making. The architecture aligns the agency with TIC 3.0, FedRAMP 20x Phase 2, and NIST 800-63 requirements, reducing compliance risk. Most importantly, the modernization improves citizen experience by delivering faster, more reliable, and more secure services.

management_and_governance: narrative: > The Management and Governance layer provides the operational orchestration that binds the five control planes into a continuously governed, auditable system. This layer is distinct from the Transport and Security tools (Cisco, Palo Alto) that move and protect traffic; instead, it focuses on ensuring that the architectural fabric remains compliant, healthy, and aligned with federal mandates over time. servicenow: role: "System of Record for Overlay Drift" narrative: > ServiceNow serves as the authoritative system of record for detecting and remediating Overlay Drift within the UIAO architecture. When the Telemetry Control Plane detects configuration deviations in the SD-WAN overlay—such as unauthorized tunnel endpoints, expired certificates, or policy mismatches against the approved baseline in program.yml— ServiceNow automatically generates a Change Request linked to the affected Configuration Item in the CMDB. The automated remediation workflow re-validates the overlay configuration, enforces corrective action, and closes the governance loop by updating the Telemetry Plane with the resolution status. This ensures that the Overlay Plane never drifts from its authorized state without detection, documentation, and remediation. fedramp_class: "Class C (Moderate)" nist_controls: - "IR-4: Incident Handling (Rev 5)" - "CA-7: Continuous Monitoring (Rev 5)" intune: role: "Identity Plane Device Trust Gatekeeper" narrative: > Microsoft Intune ensures that the Identity Control Plane only allows healthy, certified devices into the architectural fabric. Before any endpoint is granted access through the Overlay Plane, Intune validates its compliance posture: OS patch level, disk encryption status, EDR agent health, and certificate validity. This compliance signal is consumed by Entra ID Conditional Access, which enforces a device-trust gate at authentication time. Non-compliant devices are quarantined to a restricted VLAN segment managed by Cisco ISE, preventing lateral movement within the fabric. Device trust is not a one-time check but a persistent, real-time control input to the Identity Plane, ensuring continuous compliance with NIST 800-53 Rev 5 AC-19 and CM-8 requirements. fedramp_class: "Class C (Moderate)" nist_controls: - "AC-19: Access Control for Mobile Devices (Rev 5)" - "CM-8: System Component Inventory (Rev 5)"

  identity_lifecycle_scenarios:

mover_scenario: persona: "Sarah Miller, Senior Analyst" event: "Internal Transfer from Field Operations (Dept 400) to Cyber Policy (Dept 800)" narrative: > The Mover scenario demonstrates Zero-Touch internal transfers. When the HRIS updates Sarah's department ID, the Entra ID Provisioning Service detects the attribute change within one hour. The ABAC engine evaluates her identity against dynamic groups. She is automatically removed from Field Ops Users (department eq 400) and added to Cyber Policy Users (department eq 800). Access to Field Operations SharePoint is revoked while Cyber Policy Teams channel, Agency Policy Repository, and Moderate-Security Azure enclave are provisioned instantly. Conditional Access updates to require phishing-resistant MFA (PIV Card) for policy-specific resources. The Sync Orchestrator updates her ServiceNow CI record and notifies the Cyber Policy Lead via MS Teams. metrics: time_to_productivity: "Reduced from 5-7 days to less than 60 minutes" security_risk: "Eliminated access creep from accumulated permissions" admin_load: "100% reduction in manual group management for transfers" leaver_scenario: persona: "James Vance, Contractor" event: "Immediate Separation (End of Contract / High-Risk Departure)" narrative: > The Leaver scenario demonstrates the automated kill switch. When HR finalizes the separation, the HRIS pushes a status update setting Employee Status to Terminated with immediate effect. Entra ID disables the account within seconds, blocking all new sign-in sessions across M365, Azure, ServiceNow, and federated apps. Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) issues a revocation signal to all supporting apps, terminating active sessions instantly without waiting for token expiry. The Sync Orchestrator moves all assigned assets (laptop, PIV card, mobile) to In Stock Pending Recovery status in ServiceNow and generates a high-priority recovery ticket for Physical Security. Sentinel logs the disablement and monitors for failed login attempts. A final Atlas Report is sent to the Department Lead and Security via MS Teams. metrics: revocation_latency: "Reduced from hours/days to less than 120 seconds" audit_compliance: "100% automated logging for FISMA and GCC-Moderate separation" asset_accountability: "Zero-latency flagging of agency hardware for recovery"

End of Zero Trust Narrative v1.0

docs.title: ADR-0001: Restructure uiao-core as a Python Package with Type Safety and CLI
Context
docs.text:
The uiao-core repository has grown to 539+ commits with 20+ scripts in a flat scripts/ directory. Each script duplicates the load_context() function, has no type annotations, and is invoked via python scripts/foo.py with no argument parsing. This makes the codebase fragile, hard to test, and unsuitable for agency piloting where reproducibility and auditability matter.

Key pain points: - Duplicated loader logic across generate_docs.py, generate_oscal.py, generate_ssp.py, generate_poam.py, etc. - No type safety - YAML data flows through untyped dicts; typos in keys cause silent failures. - No CLI - scripts are run manually with python scripts/X.py; no --help, no argument validation. - Not installable - no pyproject.toml or package structure; cannot pip install -e . for development.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text:
Introduce a uiao_core/ Python package alongside the existing scripts/ directory (non-breaking). The package provides:

  1. uiao_core/loader.py - Single canonical load_context() with caching and validation. All scripts will eventually import from here.
  2. uiao_core/models.py - Pydantic v2 models for core data structures (ControlPlane, Parameter, ComplianceEntry, InventoryItem, etc.). Provides runtime validation and IDE autocompletion.
  3. uiao_core/cli.py - Typer-based CLI entry point with subcommands: uiao generate docs, uiao generate oscal, uiao validate, etc.
  4. pyproject.toml - PEP 621 metadata, [project.scripts] entry point, dependency declaration.
  5. py.typed marker for PEP 561 type stub support.

New dependencies: pydantic>=2.0, typer>=0.9, rich>=13.0

docs.title: Target Package Layout
docs.text:
uiao_core/
  __init__.py          # version, public API
  py.typed             # PEP 561 marker
  models.py            # Pydantic data models
  loader.py            # Canonical YAML context loader
  cli.py               # Typer CLI entry point
  generators/          # (future) refactored generators
    __init__.py
pyproject.toml         # PEP 621 package metadata
scripts/               # (unchanged, gradually migrated)

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Scripts can from uiao_core.loader import load_context - single source of truth
  • Pydantic models catch data errors at load time, not deep in generation logic
  • pip install -e . enables uiao CLI command system-wide
  • Type annotations enable mypy/pyright checks in CI
  • Agency evaluators see a professional, installable package

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Adds 3 new dependencies (pydantic, typer, rich) to requirements.txt
  • Existing scripts still work but are not yet migrated (parallel paths temporarily)
  • Pydantic models must stay in sync with YAML schema changes

docs.title: Migration Strategy
docs.text:
• Phase 1 (this PR): Create package, loader, models, CLI shell. Non-breaking.
  • Phase 2 (Week 2): Migrate scripts to import from uiao_core.loader
  • Phase 3 (Week 3): Move generator logic into uiao_core.generators/
  • Phase 4 (Week 4): Deprecate direct script invocation

docs.title: Technical Debt Notes
docs.text:
• The existing load_context() in each script merges ALL YAML files into a flat dict - key collisions are possible. The new loader should namespace by source file.
  • No tests exist for data loading; Pydantic models will serve as implicit validation tests until proper test suite is added.

docs.title: What Was Delivered
docs.text:
• ADR-0001 (package restructure decision record)
  • pyproject.toml with PEP 621 metadata and src-layout
  • requirements.txt with pinned production deps
  • src/uiao_core/ package with:
  • __init__.py, __version__.py, config.py
  • models/__init__.py, models/canon.py (Pydantic)
  • cli/__init__.py, cli/app.py (Typer)
  • generators/__init__.py, generators/ssp.py (stub)
  • py.typed (PEP 561 marker)

docs.title: What Went Well
docs.text:
1. ADR-first approach works. Writing the decision before code forced clarity.
  2. Clean package structure. The src-layout is now pip-installable.
  3. Typed from day one. py.typed + Pydantic models = good DX.
  4. CLI scaffolding is production-ready pattern. Typer + Rich.

docs.title: What Needs Improvement
docs.text:
1. No tests yet. Zero pytest coverage. Week 2 must add tests/.
  2. generators/ssp.py is a stub. The real generate_ssp.py in scripts/ was not migrated.
  3. No CI integration for the new package. pyproject.toml exists but pip install -e . is not tested in CI.
  4. config.py env vars are aspirational. UIAO_CANON_DIR etc. not used anywhere yet.
  5. Dual dependency tracking. Both requirements.txt and pyproject.toml list deps. Should consolidate.
  6. No CHANGELOG entry. Should document the restructure.

docs.title: Risk Assessment
docs.text:
• LOW: Package structure is conventional and reversible.
  • MEDIUM: Old scripts/ still works, so no regression. But drift risk if both paths exist long.
  • HIGH: No tests = no confidence gate. Must fix in Week 2.

docs.title: Week 2 Priorities
docs.text:
1. Add pytest + test fixtures for models and CLI
  2. Migrate generate_ssp.py logic into generators/ssp.py
  3. Add pip install -e . step to CI workflow
  4. Consolidate dependency management
  5. Begin ADR-0002 for validation layer

docs.title: ADR-0002: Testing Infrastructure and SSP Generator Migration
Status
docs.text: Accepted (Week 2 - 2026-03-23)

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
Week 1 (ADR-0001) established the src-layout package structure but shipped zero tests. The generators/ssp.py stub raises NotImplementedError, while the real SSP logic lives in scripts/generate_ssp.py (~180 lines). This creates two risks:

  1. No confidence gate on refactoring (any change could silently break output)
  2. Dual code paths (scripts/ vs src/) will drift over time

Additionally, CI does not exercise pip install -e . so the package is untested as an installable artifact.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text:
1. Add pytest infrastructure: tests/conftest.py with shared fixtures, tests/test_models.py, tests/test_cli.py, tests/test_generators.py
  2. Migrate scripts/generate_ssp.py logic into src/uiao_core/generators/ssp.py as importable functions (load_context(), build_set_parameters(), build_ssp_skeleton(), build_ssp())
  3. Add pip install -e . to CI so package installation is validated on every push
  4. Add pytest to requirements.txt as a dev dependency

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• First automated test coverage for the package
  • SSP generation becomes importable and testable (not just script-runnable)
  • CI validates the package is installable
  • Foundation for Week 3+ validation and further migration

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Test fixtures need real YAML data files (canon/, data/) to be meaningful
  • Migration may introduce subtle differences vs. scripts/ output
  • pytest adds a dev dependency

docs.title: Alternatives Considered
docs.text:
• unittest (rejected: pytest is standard for modern Python, better fixtures)
  • Mocking all YAML (rejected: real data tests catch schema drift)
  • Keeping scripts/ as primary (rejected: defeats Week 1 restructure purpose)

docs.title: What Was Delivered
docs.text:
• ADR-0002 (testing infrastructure and SSP migration)
  • tests/conftest.py with shared fixtures (project_root, canon_dir, data_dir, exports_dir)
  • tests/test_models.py with CanonEntry model tests and data dir assertions
  • tests/test_cli.py with CLI --version, --help, and subcommand tests
  • Full SSP generator migration: src/uiao_core/generators/ssp.py (~288 lines)
  • load_context(), build_set_parameters(), build_ssp_skeleton(), build_ssp()
  • Updated cli/app.py with --canon, --data-dir, --output options
  • New CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml (Python 3.11/3.12 matrix)
  • pip install -e . + pytest on every push
  • pytest>=7.0 added to requirements.txt

docs.title: What Went Well
docs.text:
1. SSP migration is 1:1 faithful. Same logic as scripts/, now importable.
  2. CI now validates the package is installable. Biggest risk from Week 1 eliminated.
  3. Tests exercise real CLI surface. Typer CliRunner catches import/wiring errors.
  4. ADR-first approach continues to work. Decision documented before code.

docs.title: What Needs Improvement
docs.text:
1. Tests don't run the actual SSP generator end-to-end. Would need data/ and canon/ in CI.
  2. No test for generators/ssp.py functions directly. Only CLI and models tested.
  3. scripts/generate_ssp.py not updated to shim. Dual code path still exists.
  4. Dependency duplication still present. requirements.txt + pyproject.toml.
  5. datetime.utcnow() is deprecated in Python 3.12+. Should use datetime.now(UTC).
  6. No mypy or ruff config yet. Type checking not enforced in CI.

docs.title: Risk Assessment
docs.text:
• LOW: SSP migration is backward-compatible. Old script still works.
  • MEDIUM: CI may fail on first run if trestle deps have install issues.
  • HIGH: No end-to-end SSP test = generator could silently break.

docs.title: Week 3 Priorities
docs.text:
1. Add end-to-end test for SSP generator (with real data/)
  2. Convert scripts/generate_ssp.py to a thin shim
  3. Add mypy + ruff to CI
  4. Begin ADR-0003 for remaining script migration (generate_docs, generate_oscal)
  5. Fix datetime.utcnow() deprecation

docs.title: ADR-0003: Configuration Hardening, Dependency Consolidation, Ruff/Mypy, and Full Generator Migration + Week 1/2 Debt Retirement
Status
docs.text: Proposed

docs.title: Context
docs.text: Weeks 1 and 2 established the uiao_core src-layout package with Typer CLI, Pydantic models, SSP generator migration, pytest infrastructure, and CI (Python 3.11/3.12). However, several debt items remain from Weeks 1/2 that must be retired before the repo is production-grade for FedRAMP agency piloting:

docs.title: Week 1/2 Carry-Over Debt (resolved in this sprint)
docs.text:
1. Dual dependency tracking - requirements.txt and pyproject.toml both declare deps
  2. datetime.utcnow() deprecation - deprecated in Python 3.12+
  3. Settings not injected - config.py Settings class exists but is not wired into generators or CLI
  4. No mypy / ruff / pre-commit - no static analysis or linting
  5. No end-to-end SSP generator test - unit tests exist but no full pipeline test
  6. scripts/generate_ssp.py not shimmed - legacy entry point not redirected
  7. Remaining generators not migrated - only SSP moved; docs, validate, assemble remain in scripts/

docs.title: 1. Dependency Consolidation
docs.text:
• Make pyproject.toml the single source of truth for all dependencies
  • Convert requirements.txt to dev-only ([project.optional-dependencies.dev])
  • Dev deps: pytest, ruff, mypy, pre-commit

docs.title: 2. Configuration Injection
docs.text:
• Wire Settings (from config.py) into generators/ssp.py for default paths
  • CLI options override Settings defaults
  • All file paths resolve through Settings

docs.title: 3. datetime.utcnow() Fix
docs.text:
• Replace all datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  • Applies to generators and any utility modules

docs.title: 4. Generator Migration
docs.text:
• Migrate remaining scripts into src/uiao_core/generators/:
  • docs.py - DOCX document generation
  • validate.py - OSCAL validation wrapper
  • canon_check.py - Canon YAML validation
  • Each generator follows the established pattern: pure function, Settings-aware, CLI-callable

docs.title: 5. Legacy Shims
docs.text:
• Add thin shims in scripts/ for all migrated generators
  • Each shim imports and calls the new module, prints deprecation warning
  • Zero breakage for existing users

docs.title: 6. Static Analysis
docs.text:
• Add ruff config in pyproject.toml (select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP"])
  • Add mypy config in pyproject.toml (strict = false initially, warn_return_any = true)
  • Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff and mypy hooks
  • New .github/workflows/lint.yml workflow

docs.title: 7. E2E Tests
docs.text:
• Add tests/test_e2e_ssp.py - full pipeline: load canon + data -> build SSP -> validate JSON output
  • Add tests/test_e2e_docs.py - docs generation smoke test
  • Update CI to run all tests including e2e

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Zero remaining Week 1/2 debt
  • Single dependency source (pyproject.toml)
  • Static analysis catches bugs before CI
  • Legacy scripts continue to work via shims
  • E2E tests prove the pipeline works end-to-end
  • Repo is production-grade for agency pilot evaluation

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Larger single PR / sprint scope
  • Pre-commit adds developer setup step
  • Mypy may surface type issues in existing code

docs.title: Risks
docs.text:
• Generator migration may reveal undocumented dependencies in legacy scripts
  • Ruff/mypy may require code changes beyond simple fixes

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• ADR-0001: Package Restructure
  • ADR-0002: Testing and SSP Migration
  • Grok Week 3 recommendation: "Verdict: Forward"

docs.title: Config Hardening & Dependency Consolidation
docs.text:
• pyproject.toml is now the authoritative dependency source
  • requirements.txt converted to dev-only (pip install convenience)
  • Added ruff, mypy, and pytest config sections to pyproject.toml
  • Dev extras group (pip install -e .[dev]) for developer tooling

docs.title: datetime.utcnow() Retirement
docs.text:
• Replaced deprecated datetime.utcnow() with datetime.now(timezone.utc) in SSP generator
  • Settings injection into generators/ssp.py for configurable paths

docs.title: Generator Migration (ADR-0003 Core Deliverable)
docs.text:
• docs.py: Full port of scripts/generate_docs.py with Settings injection, type hints, overlay support, and configurable template mapping
  • oscal.py: Full port of scripts/generate_oscal.py with OSCAL 1.0 Component Definition builder, inventory validation, and FedRAMP 20x alignment
  • poam.py: Full port of scripts/generate_poam.py with intelligent gap detection (maturity levels + missing KSI evidence) and OSCAL POA&M export
  • charts.py: Full port of scripts/generate_charts.py with matplotlib Agg backend, maturity radar and compliance coverage charts (USA/territories only)
  • generators/init.py: Updated with all exports (build_ssp, build_oscal, build_poam_export, build_docs)

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• Added tests/test_generators.py with end-to-end tests covering:
  • Import verification for all 4 generator modules
  • Package-level __init__ exports
  • OSCAL builder with empty and populated contexts
  • POA&M gap detection with empty and low-maturity data
  • SSP builder with empty context and tmp_path output

docs.title: CI Status
docs.text:
• All CI runs green (Python 3.11 + 3.12) through CI #13+
  • AI Security Audit passing
  • Pages deployment operational

docs.title: What Went Well
docs.text:
1. Consistent pattern: All generators follow the same architecture - Settings injection, typed signatures, configurable paths, _get_settings() helper
  2. CI stability: Every commit passed CI on first try (no regressions)
  3. Rapid execution: 4 generators + tests + init update in a single session
  4. ADR-first approach maintained: ADR-0003 was committed before implementation

docs.title: What Needs Improvement
docs.text:
1. rich_docx.py not migrated: Deferred due to heavy python-docx dependency; should be Week 4 priority
  2. Legacy shims not yet added: scripts/ still contains original files without shims pointing to new package
  3. No lint workflow yet: .github/workflows/lint.yml for ruff + mypy not created
  4. charts.py has heavy deps: matplotlib + numpy are large; should consider making them optional extras
  5. DRY violation: load_context() pattern is duplicated across oscal, poam, charts; should extract to shared utility
  6. No integration tests with real data: Tests use empty/mock contexts; need tests against actual canon YAML

docs.title: Risk Assessment
docs.text:
• Low: Generator modules all import cleanly and CI is green
  • Medium: __init__.py eagerly imports all generators, meaning jinja2/yaml must be installed; consider lazy imports
  • Medium: No ruff/mypy enforcement yet means type errors could accumulate

docs.title: Week 4 Priorities
docs.text:
1. Migrate generate_rich_docx.py into package
  2. Add thin legacy shims in scripts/ for backward compatibility
  3. Create .github/workflows/lint.yml (ruff check + mypy)
  4. Extract shared load_context() into uiao_core.generators._common
  5. Add integration tests with actual canon YAML data
  6. Begin Week 4 ADR: CI/CD hardening, artifact publishing, and documentation site improvements

docs.title: ADR-0004: Final Migration, Lint Enforcement, DRY Refactoring, and Agency Pilot Packaging
Status
docs.text: Accepted

docs.title: Context
docs.text:
Weeks 1-3 of the UIAO-Core modernization established: - Week 1 (ADR-0001): src-layout package structure, Typer CLI, Pydantic models, generator stubs. - Week 2 (ADR-0002): pytest infrastructure, CI pipeline, SSP generator migration. - Week 3 (ADR-0003): Config hardening, dependency consolidation, datetime fix, migration of docs/oscal/poam/charts generators, 11 e2e tests.

The Week 3 self-critique (0003-week3-critique.md) and external assessment (Grade: B+ 88/100) identified the following remaining technical debt:

  1. generate_rich_docx.py and trestle scripts (validate_with_trestle.py, assemble_with_trestle.py) still in legacy /scripts/ -- not yet ported.
  2. Legacy shims missing -- external callers/CI could break if they call old scripts directly.
  3. No lint workflow -- ruff/mypy are configured in pyproject.toml but not enforced in CI.
  4. load_context() duplicated across oscal.py, poam.py, and charts.py (DRY violation).
  5. Settings injection incomplete -- not all generators and CLI commands use the Pydantic Settings model.
  6. No true integration tests against real canon YAML data.

This ADR retires all remaining Week 1-3 debt and brings the repository to agency-pilot readiness.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: We will execute the following changes in priority order:

docs.title: 1. DRY Refactor: Shared Context Loader
docs.text: Extract the duplicated load_context() function into src/uiao_core/utils/context.py and update all generators (oscal, poam, charts) to import from the shared module.

docs.title: 2. Final Generator Migrations
docs.text:
• Rich DOCX: Port scripts/generate_rich_docx.py to src/uiao_core/generators/rich_docx.py with Settings injection, type hints, and python-docx integration.
  • Trestle: Combine scripts/validate_with_trestle.py and scripts/assemble_with_trestle.py into src/uiao_core/generators/trestle.py with two public functions: validate_with_trestle() and assemble_with_trestle().

docs.title: 3. Legacy Shims
docs.text: Add thin wrapper scripts in scripts/ that import and call the new uiao_core package functions. This ensures zero-downtime for any external callers or CI jobs referencing the old script paths.

docs.title: 4. Complete Settings Injection
docs.text: Ensure every generator, CLI command, and model uses the Pydantic Settings class for configuration (canon paths, output directories, template paths).

docs.title: 5. Lint Enforcement
docs.text:
• Create .github/workflows/lint.yml running ruff check + mypy on every push/PR.
  • Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff, mypy, and trailing-whitespace hooks.

docs.title: 6. Integration Tests
docs.text: Add tests/test_integration.py with end-to-end tests using real canon YAML fixtures and tmp_path, covering at least one test per generator.

docs.title: 7. Agency Pilot Packaging
docs.text:
• Update README.md and USAGE.md with complete CLI examples and "Agency Pilot Quickstart" section.
  • Add LICENSE (MIT), CONTRIBUTING.md.
  • Add docs/pilot-artifacts/ with example outputs and a FedRAMP Pilot Checklist.
  • Verify sdist/wheel build instructions work.

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Zero remaining technical debt from the original 4-week plan.
  • Full CI enforcement -- lint, type checking, and tests run automatically.
  • DRY codebase -- shared utilities eliminate duplication.
  • Agency-pilot ready -- any federal agency can pip install, run CLI, and generate FedRAMP artifacts in under 5 minutes.
  • Backward compatible -- legacy shims preserve old script paths.

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Additional maintenance burden for legacy shims (mitigated by deprecation warnings).
  • Pre-commit hooks add friction for contributors (mitigated by clear CONTRIBUTING.md docs).

docs.title: Risks
docs.text:
• Rich DOCX and trestle generators depend on external packages (python-docx, compliance-trestle) that may have version conflicts. Mitigated by pinning in pyproject.toml extras.
  • Integration tests with real canon data may be brittle if canon schema changes. Mitigated by using minimal fixture data.

docs.title: Alternatives Considered
docs.text:
1. Skip legacy shims: Would break backward compatibility for any existing automation. Rejected.
  2. Defer lint enforcement to a separate PR: Would leave the debt open. Rejected -- this ADR closes everything.
  3. Keep load_context() per-module: Simpler but violates DRY and increases maintenance cost. Rejected.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• ADR-0001: Package Restructure (Week 1)
  • ADR-0002: Testing and SSP Migration (Week 2)
  • ADR-0003: Config Hardening, Dependency Consolidation, Generator Migration (Week 3)
  • 0001-week1-critique.md, 0002-week2-critique.md, 0003-week3-critique.md
  • External assessment: B+ (88/100) with explicit debt items listed above

docs.title: Week 4 Critique: Final Migration, Lint, DRY, and Pilot Packaging
docs.text: Date: 2026-03-23 ADR: ADR-0004 Status: Complete

docs.title: Scope of Work
docs.text:
Week 4 targeted the final technical debt items identified in Weeks 1-3 critiques and Grok's external assessment. The deliverables were:

  1. ADR-0004 - Decision record for DRY refactor, migration, lint, and packaging
  2. DRY Refactor - Extract load_context(), load_canon(), get_settings() into utils/context.py
  3. Generator Updates - All 5 generators (oscal, poam, charts, ssp, docs) refactored to use shared utils
  4. Rich DOCX Migration - scripts/generate_rich_docx.py ported to generators/rich_docx.py
  5. Trestle Migration - scripts/assemble_with_trestle.py ported to generators/trestle.py
  6. Legacy Shims - Scripts in scripts/ now import from package generators
  7. Lint Workflow - ci/lint.yml with ruff and mypy
  8. Pre-commit - .pre-commit-config.yaml with ruff hooks
  9. Integration Tests - test_integration.py verifying imports and API signatures
  10. Packaging - docxtpl added to pyproject.toml dependencies

docs.title: What Went Well
docs.text:
• DRY elimination was clean. The shared utils/context.py module reduced ~150 lines of duplicated context-loading logic across 5 generators to a single source of truth.
  • CI stayed green throughout. Every commit (CI #16 through #31) passed, demonstrating incremental, non-breaking changes.
  • Conventional commits maintained throughout, enabling automated changelog generation.
  • ADR-first discipline held: ADR-0004 was committed before any code changes.
  • Settings injection via pydantic-settings preserved .env-less CI compatibility with _env_file=None fallback.

docs.title: What Could Be Improved
docs.text:
• No end-to-end generation test. Integration tests verify imports and signatures but do not exercise actual document generation against test fixtures. A tests/data/ fixture with minimal canon YAML would enable smoke tests.
  • Rich DOCX hardcoded visual filenames. The evidence_map and visuals lists in rich_docx.py contain hardcoded image filenames rather than reading from canon data. This should be data-driven in a future iteration.
  • Type annotations incomplete. While mypy is configured, disallow_untyped_defs = false means many functions lack type hints. Week 5 should tighten this.
  • No py.typed marker. The package does not yet declare PEP 561 compliance.
  • Legacy shims still exist. The scripts/ shims should eventually be deprecated with clear messaging directing users to uiao CLI or direct package imports.

docs.title: Remaining Technical Debt
docs.text: Item Priority Notes E2E generation smoke tests High Need test canon fixtures Tighten mypy (disallow_untyped_defs = true) Medium Incremental per-module Data-driven visual references in rich_docx Medium Read from canon YAML Add py.typed marker Low PEP 561 compliance Deprecation warnings in legacy shims Low Warn users to migrate SBOM / license scanning Low Supply chain security

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
• Commits this week: 15 (CI #16 through #31)
  • CI pass rate: 100% on CI workflow
  • New files: 6 (utils/__init__.py, utils/context.py, generators/rich_docx.py, generators/trestle.py, test_integration.py, .pre-commit-config.yaml)
  • Modified files: 8 (5 generators, pyproject.toml, 2 script shims)
  • Lines of duplication removed: ~150 (estimated across generators)

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: Week 4 successfully retired the remaining Week 1-3 technical debt. The codebase now has a single shared context loader, all generators live in the installable package, linting and pre-commit enforce code quality, and integration tests verify public APIs. The project is in a solid position for a v0.2.0 milestone release with the remaining debt items tracked above as post-release improvements.

docs.title: ADR-0005: Dev Repo Promotion Workflow
docs.text: Status: Accepted Date: 2026-03-31 Decision Makers: Michael (UIAO Architect) Source: Copilot session "Creating a Dev Repo for UIAO Core"

docs.title: Context
docs.text: UIAO Core requires a safe development sandbox for experimentation, compiler integration, directory restructuring, and docs evolution without polluting the authoritative canonical repo. The question was whether to use feature branches within uiao-core or create a separate uiao-core-dev repository.

docs.title: Decision
docs.text: Create uiao-core-dev as a temporary staging repo using One-Way Canonical Sync (Model 1).

docs.title: Sync Model
docs.text:
• uiao-core is the authoritative canon (source of truth)
  • uiao-core-dev regularly pulls from uiao-core
  • uiao-core-dev never pushes back automatically
  • Promotion happens manually and intentionally via PR

docs.title: Rejected Alternatives
docs.text: Model Reason for Rejection Two-Way Sync Creates merge conflicts, drift, governance ambiguity; violates NO-HALLUCINATION PROTOCOL Long-Running Feature Branch Acceptable but less clean separation than dedicated repo

docs.title: Step 1: Prepare Promotion Candidate
docs.text: In uiao-core-dev: - Ensure experimental content is either promoted to canonical form or moved to /experiments - Run drift checks - Run schema validation - Run compiler validation - Run governance invariants

docs.title: Step 2: Create Promotion Branch
docs.text: git checkout -b promote-YYYYMMDD

docs.title: Step 3: Sync with Core
docs.text:
git fetch core
git rebase core/main

Resolve conflicts in favor of the canon unless explicitly intended.

docs.title: Step 4: Open Promotion PR
docs.text: Open PR from uiao-core-dev -> uiao-core with: - Summary of changes - Validation results - Drift report - Compiler test results - Governance checklist

docs.title: Step 5: Human Approval
docs.text: The human (Michael) approves the PR.

docs.title: Step 6: Merge into Canon
docs.text: Once merged: - uiao-core becomes the new authoritative truth - uiao-core-dev pulls from it again to reset drift

docs.title: uiao-core (Authoritative Canon)
docs.text:
• main only
  • No feature branches
  • No drafts or experiments
  • No direct commits except via promotion

docs.title: uiao-core-dev (Development Sandbox)
docs.text: Branch Pattern Purpose main Active development feature/* Experimental branches structure/* Directory evolution compiler/* Compiler changes migration/* Restructuring promote/* Promotion candidates

docs.title: GitHub Actions Required
docs.text: Action Scope Purpose drift-check.yml Both repos Validates directory structure against canonical schema, ensures no forbidden dirs, no version refs schema-validation.yml Both repos Validates all YAML/JSON against canonical schemas compiler-validation.yml Both repos Runs Document Compiler, ensures clean builds promotion-gate.yml Core only Blocks PRs unless drift, schema, compiler, and governance checks all pass sync-reminder.yml Dev only Weekly job alerting if uiao-core-dev is behind uiao-core by more than N commits

docs.title: Positive
docs.text:
• Clean separation between canonical and experimental surfaces
  • Drift-resistant canon preserved
  • Aligns with federal modernization sandbox/pre-prod/prod pipeline model
  • Deterministic governance with zero accidental overwrites

docs.title: Negative
docs.text:
• Requires maintaining two repositories
  • Additional overhead for sync and promotion workflows
  • Dev repo is temporary and should be retired once canon stabilizes

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• COPILOT-INTEGRATION-LOG.md - Thread 1
  • UIAO-MEMORY.md - Canonical governance principles
